status: P1 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 7 / SEQUENCE OR SUPPLIER-EQUIVALENCE BLOCK ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Supplier-named hepatic short regulatory peptide family
mechanism_summary: No canonical mechanism is assigned. The product name suggests an organ-targeted commercial positioning, but exact sequence, target and pharmacology require direct primary-source and analytical closure.
evidence_maturity: Regional short-peptide, cell, animal or limited human literature may exist under this name or related names. Evidence is not attributed to the catalogue product until chemical identity and source linkage are confirmed.
regulatory_headline: IDENTITY-SENSITIVE SUPPLIER PRODUCT FAMILY / NO UNIVERSAL APPROVAL OR ORGAN-TREATMENT CLAIM
certification_blockers: Molecular formula, mass and reference standard
Current product- and jurisdiction-specific regulatory status
closure_class: KEDA TETRAPEPTIDE / HEPATIC POSITIONING AND HUMAN TRANSLATION
closure_state: PARTIAL CLOSURE — LIVAGEN LITERATURE IDENTITY LYS-GLU-ASP-ALA (KEDA) AND IN-VITRO/ANIMAL PROGRAMME IDENTIFIED; LI20 TERMINI, SALT, ANALYTICAL IDENTITY, STERILITY AND CONTROLLED HUMAN BENEFIT OPEN
authoritative_product_boundary: Primary literature identifies Livagen as Lys-Glu-Asp-Ala (KEDA) and reports in-vitro human-serum enzyme experiments, animal digestive-enzyme studies and chromatin/cell research. These records do not establish an authorised liver medicine or analytical and clinical equivalence of LI20.
critical_closure_controls: KEDA literature identity is not LI20 analytical proof

Human-serum in-vitro experiments are not human treatment evidence
